title: Thelypteris gracilis
intro: Thelypteris gracilis, also known as the Slender Lady Fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris gracilis include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Thelypteris gracilis is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and it may also be used as a component in traditional remedies such as salves and infusions for topical application.
taxonomy: Thelypteris gracilis belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae and is classified within the genus Thelypteris. This genus is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. Thelypteris gracilis is native to North America and is often found growing in woodland areas. The species is also known as the slender lady fern due to its slender fronds.
